### Items outside the comfort range

- `Q2-ALIGNMENT-01-L` (leader, grade 4.8): I reinforce the behaviors that match what we say matters most.
- `Q2-ALIGNMENT-03-T` (team_member, grade 15.8): Success is defined consistently across the organization.
- `Q2-ALIGNMENT-05-T` (team_member, grade 12.7): Different parts of the organization seem to be working toward different priorities.
- `Q2-CLARITY-01-L` (leader, grade 1.9): When plans change, I explain what changed and what stays the same.
- `Q2-CLARITY-02-T` (team_member, grade 3.7): I understand what success looks like for my work right now.
- `Q2-CLARITY-04-T` (team_member, grade 5.8): I can explain how my work connects to the team’s current priorities.
- `Q2-COMMUNICATION-01-T` (team_member, grade 4.9): I receive the information I need in time to do my job well.
- `Q2-CONSISTENCY-02-T` (team_member, grade 14.1): Leadership standards are applied consistently across people and situations.
- `Q2-CONSISTENCY-03-T` (team_member, grade 4.9): The way issues are handled depends on timing, mood, or who is involved.
- `Q2-LEADERSHIP-DRIFT-03-T` (team_member, grade 3.7): It takes longer to resolve issues now than it used to.
- `Q2-LEADERSHIP-DRIFT-05-L` (leader, grade 5.2): I follow the same leadership habits now as I did when things were going well.
- `Q2-LEADERSHIP-DRIFT-05-T` (team_member, grade 3.7): Concerns are addressed earlier now than they were in the past.
- `Q2-STABILITY-02-T` (team_member, grade 5.8): Small issues tend to feel heavier than they should in this environment.
- `Q2-STABILITY-03-T` (team_member, grade 4.8): Changes are introduced with enough lead time to adjust well.
- `Q2-STABILITY-04-T` (team_member, grade 14.4): When pressure increases, communication stays clear enough to avoid unnecessary friction.
- `Q2-TRUST-04-L` (leader, grade 5.8): I handle mistakes in a way that supports learning rather than fear.
